fault_hypothesis: On line 75, the comparison uses strict `>` instead of `>=`, so when `used + request` exactly equals `effective_cap` the function incorrectly returns `True` instead of `False`.

constraints.intent: The strict-greater-than branch condition must be equivalent to greater-than-or-equal when they diverge at the boundary (used+request == effective_cap), catching the off-by-one.

spec.expr: not (used__1 + request == effective_cap__1) or (used__1 + request >= effective_cap__1)

constraints.intent: Immediately after computing used, if used+request equals effective_cap then the deny condition (>=) must trigger.
In the else branch (returning True), used+request must be strictly less than effective_cap, not merely less-than-or-equal.
The return value should be True only when used+request is strictly less than effective_cap.
